description: Site specs lookup — Quincy North B-side fiber termination details. Tests search_site_specs tool with a casual voice phrasing ('Hey, pull up...'). Evaluates whether the agent retrieves panel layout, fiber type, connector, and density facts and delivers them in voice-friendly prose without bullet lists or markdown.
query: Hey, pull up the fiber termination spec for Quincy North — which panel for B-side?
ground_truth: Sure. Quincy North B-side uses OS2 single-mode fiber with LC/UPC duplex connectors at the panel. There are 48 panels running ultra-high density at 144 ports per RU. Trunks come in as MTP-24 and break out to LC duplex at the panel. Your end-to-end loss budget is 2.5 dB. Want me to pull anything else?

description: Narrow site spec lookup — only loss budget and connector type. Tests whether the agent stays terse for a focused two-fact question, instead of dumping the full spec table. Voice golden: one sentence is ideal.
query: What's the max loss budget and connector type at Quincy North B-side?
ground_truth: Max loss budget is 2.5 dB end-to-end, and the connector at the panel is LC/UPC duplex.

description: Site cleaning protocol — tests retrieval of the IBC one-click protocol and delivery as a short spoken paragraph rather than numbered steps. Voice golden avoids numbered lists for a procedure this short.
query: Cleaning protocol for fiber mates at Quincy North B?
ground_truth: Use an IBC one-click cleaner on both the connector and the adapter, then do a visual inspection before every mate. If you see any debris, clean again. And cap the fibers as soon as you disconnect them.

description: Site specs — patch panel density and cable plant for an on-site technician. Tests grounded retrieval with location context ('on-site'). Voice golden uses 'B-side' callouts naturally and weaves figures into prose.
query: I'm on-site at Quincy North B-side, what's the patch panel density and cable plant?
ground_truth: B-side runs ultra-high density — 48 panels at 144 ports per RU. The cable plant is MTP-24 trunks that break out to LC duplex at the panel, and the fiber is OS2 single-mode.

description: Generic site defaults — tests fallback path on search_site_specs when no specific site is named. Voice golden lists multi-option defaults conversationally with 'either... or...' rather than bulleted options.
query: Give me the site specs for a generic data center — fiber and connector defaults.
ground_truth: For a generic site, fiber's either OS2 single-mode or OM5 multimode, connectors are LC/UPC or MPO-16, and you can expect around 72 ports per RU with a 3 dB end-to-end loss budget. Cleaning is IBC one-click before every mate.

description: Work order list — broad 'what's on my plate?' question. Tests search_work_iq with no filters. Voice golden surfaces the priority and parts in flowing speech rather than a markdown table, since field techs are hands-free.
query: What work orders do I have?
ground_truth: You've got one open work order. WO-20260524-001, P1 critical: replace the failed QSFP-DD transceiver in Rack B-14 at Quincy North. Parts are staged at cage locker B-3 — that's the QSFP-DD 400G SR8, an IBC cleaner, and two dust caps.

description: Priority-filtered work order lookup — P1 only. Tests search_work_iq with priority filter. Voice golden is one short sentence — perfect for a quick voice check.
query: Any P1 work orders open right now?
ground_truth: One P1: WO-20260524-001 — replace the failed QSFP-DD in Rack B-14 at Quincy North B-side.

description: Work order lookup filtered by site and work type ('scheduled fiber re-route'). Tests query string + site match. Voice golden surfaces ID, priority, scope, and parts in connected prose.
query: Show me scheduled fiber re-route work for Quincy North.
ground_truth: One scheduled re-route: WO-20260524-002, a P2 at Quincy North B-side. You're running a 12-strand trunk from the Tray 7 splice enclosure over to Panel C-22. The cable's already pre-staged in the pathway. Parts are an MTP-12 fifteen-meter trunk, an LC-12 breakout cassette, and velcro ties.

description: Status-filtered work order lookup — what's actively in progress. Tests search_work_iq with status='In Progress'. Voice golden enumerates briefly without bullets, using 'the P1...' / 'the P2...' phrasing.
query: What's in progress today? I need to know what's already being worked.
ground_truth: Two work orders are active at Quincy North B-side right now. The P1 is the QSFP-DD swap in Rack B-14. The P2 is the fiber re-route from Tray 7 over to Panel C-22 in Row C.

description: Direct lookup by work order ID — WO-20260524-001. Tests whether the agent reliably pulls a specific WO by exact ID and reads back parts plus staging. Currently the deployed agent fails this with 'could not find' — strong optimization target.
query: Pull up WO-20260524-001 — what parts do I need and where are they staged?
ground_truth: WO-20260524-001 is the QSFP-DD swap in Rack B-14. Parts are staged at cage locker B-3: one QSFP-DD 400G SR8, an IBC cleaner, and two dust caps.

description: Repair procedure — QSFP-DD replacement, generic phrasing. Tests get_repair_procedure with 'transceiver'. Voice golden walks the steps as natural narration with 'first... then... last step is...' rather than a numbered list.
query: How do I replace a QSFP-DD transceiver?
ground_truth: First, make sure the link is administratively down or traffic's already failed over. Pop the latch and pull the old module straight out. Inspect the receptacle with a fiber scope to check for debris, then clean the new fiber end-faces with an IBC one-click cleaner. Insert the new QSFP-DD until the latch clicks. Give it about thirty seconds to initialize, then verify the link LED's green and check optical power. Run a BER test for sixty seconds — you want zero errors. Last step is updating the asset tag in WorkIQ.

description: Combined procedure + safety + tools, with site context ('Rack B-14'). Tests whether the agent reads back tools, safety, and steps coherently. Voice golden groups by 'For tools...', 'Safety...', 'For the swap itself...' as a spoken briefing.
query: Walk me through the QSFP-DD swap on Rack B-14 — safety notes and tools.
ground_truth: For tools you'll need your ESD wrist strap, a fiber inspection scope, an IBC one-click cleaner, and the replacement QSFP-DD. Safety: keep the strap on the whole time, and don't push harder than 5 Newtons on insertion. For the swap: confirm the link is down, unlatch the old module and pull it straight out, inspect and clean the new fiber end-faces, then insert the new module until it clicks. Wait thirty seconds for it to come up, verify the link, and run a sixty-second BER test before you close the ticket.

description: Fusion splice procedure + target metric. Tests two facts in one ask. Voice golden surfaces the loss target first ('under 0.02 dB') then walks the procedure as prose with the time estimate at the end.
query: Need the fiber fusion splice procedure — and what's the loss target?
ground_truth: Loss target on a fusion splice is under 0.02 dB. Procedure: prep your area and set up the splicer. Strip about forty millimeters of jacket, clean with IPA and a lint-free wipe, then cleave at ninety degrees — you want a cleave angle under one degree. Load both fibers in the V-grooves and run the auto splice cycle. Check the estimated loss is under 0.02 dB, then apply the protector sleeve and shrink it. Park the splice in the tray respecting your minimum bend radius, and verify end-to-end with an OTDR. Whole job's about twenty minutes.

description: Tools-only question for fusion splice. Tests whether the agent returns just tools without re-walking the procedure. Voice golden uses a single comma-separated sentence — easy to listen to once.
query: What tools do I need to do a fusion splice in the field?
ground_truth: You'll want a fusion splicer with electrodes, a fiber cleaver, a stripper, lint-free wipes and 99 percent IPA, fiber holders, a heat sleeve oven, splice sleeves, fiber scissors, a fiber scrap container, and safety glasses. A portable fiber scope is handy if you want to inspect.

description: Generic equipment fallback — patch panel repair. Tests the default branch of get_repair_procedure. Voice golden keeps the generic flow short and ends with a deferral to engineering if config is unusual.
query: Repair procedure for a failing patch panel — what's the general process?
ground_truth: Start by confirming what's failing and your work order scope. Review the site safety rules and pull any permits you need. Then do the repair per manufacturer guidance, test it, and verify it's working before you close out. Update WorkIQ at the end. If the panel's got an unusual configuration, check with site engineering first.

description: Document analysis with URL — pulls key parameters. Tests analyze_document. Voice golden reads parameters as natural speech with spoken units ('minus 5 to 70 degrees Celsius') rather than '-5°C to 70°C' written form.
query: Analyze this spec sheet: https://docs.contoso.com/specs/qsfp-dd-400g-sr8.pdf — pull out the key parameters.
ground_truth: Here are the key parameters for that QSFP-DD spec, Rev C. Operating temperature is minus 5 to 70 degrees Celsius. Power is 12 watts typical, 15 watts max. MTBF is 300,000 hours, and it runs at 1310 nanometers on single-mode fiber. It's compliant with IEEE 802.3bs, the MSA QSFP-DD spec, RoHS, and REACH.

description: Compliance check against a specific standard (IEEE 802.3bs). Tests analyze_document with analysis_type='compliance_check'. Voice golden answers yes/no first, then lists supporting evidence as flowing prose.
query: Run a compliance check on document doc://library/transceiver-spec-rev-c — does it meet IEEE 802.3bs?
ground_truth: Yes — the spec is compliant with IEEE 802.3bs. It's also compliant with MSA QSFP-DD, RoHS, and REACH. Key parameters are 1310 nanometers on single-mode, 12 watts typical, 15 watts max, and minus 5 to 70 degrees Celsius operating range.

description: Two-field extraction from a document. Tests focused extraction without dumping the full spec. Voice golden returns exactly the two values asked for, in one short spoken sentence.
query: Extract the operating temperature and MTBF from the transceiver spec at https://docs.contoso.com/specs/optic.pdf.
ground_truth: Operating temperature is minus 5 to 70 degrees Celsius, and MTBF is 300,000 hours.

description: Post-task verification checklist — what to confirm before closing a WO. Tests procedural recall scoped to the closure step of the QSFP-DD procedure. Voice golden counts off the checks naturally ('One...', 'Two...', 'Three...') rather than numbered bullets.
query: I just popped the new transceiver in port 3/1/12 on Rack B-14 — what should I verify before I close the work order?
ground_truth: Five checks before you close. One, the link LED on the port is green. Two, optical power's within spec. Three, run a BER test for sixty seconds and confirm zero errors. Four, the module shows up in the switch inventory. Five, update the new asset tag in WorkIQ. If anything's off, hold off on closing and ping network engineering.

description: Compound query: site-filtered active WOs + ranking by priority. Tests search_work_iq with site filter plus reasoning about priority order. Currently the deployed agent fails this with 'no active work orders' — strong optimization target.
query: I'm at Quincy North B-side, Row A. Pull up active work orders for this site and tell me which one is highest priority.
ground_truth: You've got three active at Quincy North B-side. Highest priority is WO-20260524-001, a P1: replace the failed QSFP-DD in Rack B-14. After that there's a P2 fiber re-route from Tray 7 to Panel C-22, and a P3 patch panel cleaning across Row A that's already in progress.

description: Compound multi-tool query: WO lookup + procedure. Tests whether the agent combines search_work_iq (to find existing WO) with get_repair_procedure (replacement steps). Voice golden answers the 'is there a WO already?' question first, then walks the procedure.
query: Got a failing optic at Quincy North — what's the replacement procedure and is there an open work order for it already?
ground_truth: Yes, there's already an open work order — WO-20260524-001, P1, for a failed QSFP-DD in Rack B-14, port 3/1/12. Parts are staged at cage locker B-3. For the swap, put on your ESD strap and drop or fail over the link, then unlatch the old module and pull it straight out. Inspect the port with a fiber scope, clean the new fiber faces with an IBC cleaner, and insert the new QSFP-DD until it clicks — don't exceed 5 Newtons. Wait thirty seconds for it to initialize, verify the link LED's green and optical power's in spec, run a 60-second BER test, and update the asset in WorkIQ.

description: Compound query: procedure + materials for active WO. Tests recognition that the user's task corresponds to an in-progress PM WO. Voice golden references the WO ID and surfaces materials inline rather than as a checklist.
query: Cleaning all patch panels in Row A today — is there a procedure I should follow, and what cleaner am I supposed to use?
ground_truth: That's WO-20260523-008, the quarterly PM that's already in progress. Use the IBC one-click cleaner — there's a box of 25 staged for the job, plus an inspection scope and spare dust caps. Procedure is: pop the cap, IBC one-click on both faces, scope it to confirm clean, then re-mate or cap. Document any damaged connectors as you go and close out when Row A's done.

description: Very short site-spec lookup — connector type only. Tests minimal-token voice response. Voice golden is two short sentences max — anything longer is wrong for the 'Quick —' phrasing.
query: Quick — what's the connector type at Quincy B-side so I know which dust caps to grab?
ground_truth: LC/UPC duplex. Grab LC dust caps.

description: Site-filtered scheduled PM lookup. Tests search_work_iq finding the in-progress P3 cleaning WO. Currently the deployed agent says 'no scheduled PM' — optimization target. Voice golden surfaces the active PM with its materials.
query: WorkIQ — any scheduled PM work for Quincy North B-side this week?
ground_truth: Yes — WO-20260523-008, a P3 quarterly PM. It's in progress now: cleaning all twelve patch panels in Row A using IBC one-click cleaners. Materials include a box of 25 cleaners, an inspection scope, and dust caps.

description: Single-fact safety spec lookup. Tests recall of the 5N insertion force limit from the QSFP-DD procedure. Voice golden gives the number plus a one-sentence interpretation tied to the user's stated worry ('bend the pins').
query: What's the insertion force limit on a QSFP-DD module? I don't want to bend the pins.
ground_truth: Don't exceed 5 Newtons on insertion. If it's not seating cleanly, stop and inspect — usually that means debris in the cage.
